lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਬਿਦਿਆ ਬਿਕ੍ਰਦਾ ਬਿਖੁ ਖਟੇ ਬਿਖੁ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh bidiaa bikradaa bikh khatte bikh khaae |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh sells his knowledge; he earns poison, and eats poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਦਿਆ ਸੋਧੈ ਤਤੁ ਲਹੈ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bidiaa sodhai tat lahai raam naam liv laae |
lines.english: Considering his knowledge, he finds the essence of reality, and lovingly focuses his attention on the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਚੀਨਈ ਸੂਝ ਬੂਝ ਨਹ ਕਾਇ ॥੫੩॥
lines.translit: moorakh sabad na cheenee soojh boojh neh kaae |53|
lines.english: The fool does not think of the Word of the Shabad. He has no understanding, no comprehension. ||53||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਧਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਆਖੀਐ ਚਾਟੜਿਆ ਮਤਿ ਦੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: paadhaa guramukh aakheeai chaattarriaa mat dee |
lines.english: That Pandit is called Gurmukh, who imparts understanding to his students.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੀ ਪਟੀ ਸਚੁ ਮਨਿ ਪੜੀਐ ਸਬਦੁ ਸੁ ਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: sachee pattee sach man parreeai sabad su saar |
lines.english: With the true notebook of the true mind, study the most sublime Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਸਮਾਲਹੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਸੰਗਰਹੁ ਲਾਹਾ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਲੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: naam samaalahu naam sangarahu laahaa jag meh lee |
lines.english: Contemplate the Naam, the Name of the Lord; gather in the Naam, and earn the true profit in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋ ਪੜਿਆ ਸੋ ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਬੀਨਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਗਲਿ ਹਾਰੁ ॥੫੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak so parriaa so panddit beenaa jis raam naam gal haar |54|1|
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone is learned, and he alone is a wise Pandit, who wears the necklace of the Lord's Name. ||54||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮਕਲੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਸਿਧ ਗੋਸਟਿ ॥
lines.translit: raamakalee mahalaa 1 sidh gosatt |
lines.english: Raamkalee, First Mehl, Sidh Gosht ~ Conversations With The Siddhas:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਆਗੈ ਰਹਰਾਸਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਸਾਚਾ ਅਪਰ ਅਪਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: tis aagai raharaas hamaaree saachaa apar apaaro |
lines.english: I offer my salutation to the One who is true, infinite and incomparably beautiful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਧ ਸਭਾ ਕਰਿ ਆਸਣਿ ਬੈਠੇ ਸੰਤ ਸਭਾ ਜੈਕਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: sidh sabhaa kar aasan baitthe sant sabhaa jaikaaro |
lines.english: The Siddhas formed an assembly; sitting in their Yogic postures, they shouted, "Salute this gathering of Saints."

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੰਤੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਸਹਜ ਭਾਇ ਜਸੁ ਲੇਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak sant milai sach paaeeai sehaj bhaae jas leo |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, meeting with the Saints, Truth is obtained, and one is spontaneously blessed with distinction. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਸਤਕੁ ਕਾਟਿ ਧਰੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਗੈ ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਆਗੈ ਦੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: masatak kaatt dharee tis aagai tan man aagai deo |
lines.english: I cut off my head, and offer it to Him; I dedicate my body and mind to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਭਵੀਐ ਸਚਿ ਸੂਚਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa bhaveeai sach soochaa hoe |
lines.english: What is the use of wandering around? Purity comes only through Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਤੁਮੇ ਕਿਆ ਨਾਉ ਤੁਮਾਰਾ ਕਉਨੁ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਕਉਨੁ ਸੁਆਓ ॥
lines.translit: kavan tume kiaa naau tumaaraa kaun maarag kaun suaao |
lines.english: Who are you? What is your name? What is your way? What is your goal?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚ ਸਬਦ ਬਿਨੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saach sabad bin mukat na koe |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Without the True Word of the Shabad, no one finds liberation. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੁ ਕਹਉ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਹਉ ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਓ ॥
lines.translit: saach khau aradaas hamaaree hau sant janaa bal jaao |
lines.english: We pray that you will answer us truthfully; we are a sacrifice to the humble Saints.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹ ਬੈਸਹੁ ਕਹ ਰਹੀਐ ਬਾਲੇ ਕਹ ਆਵਹੁ ਕਹ ਜਾਹੋ ॥
lines.translit: keh baisahu keh raheeai baale keh aavahu keh jaaho |
lines.english: Where is your seat? Where do you live, boy? Where did you come from, and where are you going?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਬੋਲੈ ਸੁਣਿ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਕਿਆ ਤੁਮਾਰਾ ਰਾਹੋ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak bolai sun bairaagee kiaa tumaaraa raaho |2|
lines.english: Tell us, Nanak - the detached Siddhas wait to hear your reply. What is your path?" ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਬੈਸਿ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਰਹੀਐ ਚਾਲਹਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਭਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt bais nirantar raheeai chaaleh satigur bhaae |
lines.english: He dwells deep within the nucleus of each and every heart. This is my seat and my home. I walk in harmony with the Will of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜੇ ਆਏ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਸਿਧਾਏ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਦਾ ਰਜਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: sahaje aae hukam sidhaae naanak sadaa rajaae |
lines.english: I came from the Celestial Lord God; I go wherever He orders me to go. I am Nanak, forever under the Command of His Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਣਿ ਬੈਸਣਿ ਥਿਰੁ ਨਾਰਾਇਣੁ ਐਸੀ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: aasan baisan thir naaraaein aisee guramat paae |
lines.english: I sit in the posture of the eternal, imperishable Lord. These are the Teachings I have received from the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਆਪੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਸਚੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh boojhai aap pachhaanai sache sach samaae |3|
lines.english: As Gurmukh, I have come to understand and realize myself; I merge in the Truest of the True. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਪਟੁ ਬੋਲੈ ਅਉਧੂ ਨਾਨਕ ਦੇਹੁ ਸਚਾ ਬੀਚਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: charapatt bolai aaudhoo naanak dehu sachaa beechaaro |
lines.english: Charpat the Yogi says, "O Nanak, think it over, and give us your true reply."

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਨੀਆ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਦੁਤਰੁ ਕਹੀਐ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਪਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: duneea saagar dutar kaheeai kiau kar paaeeai paaro |
lines.english: "The world-ocean is treacherous and impassable; how can one cross over?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਆਖੈ ਆਪੇ ਸਮਝੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਕਿਆ ਉਤਰੁ ਦੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: aape aakhai aape samajhai tis kiaa utar deejai |
lines.english: What answer can I give to someone, who claims to understand himself?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੁ ਕਹਹੁ ਤੁਮ ਪਾਰਗਰਾਮੀ ਤੁਝੁ ਕਿਆ ਬੈਸਣੁ ਦੀਜੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: saach kahahu tum paaragaraamee tujh kiaa baisan deejai |4|
lines.english: I speak the Truth; if you have already crossed over, how can I argue with you? ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਮਹਿ ਕਮਲੁ ਨਿਰਾਲਮੁ ਮੁਰਗਾਈ ਨੈ ਸਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: jaise jal meh kamal niraalam muragaaee nai saane |
lines.english: The lotus flower floats untouched upon the surface of the water, and the duck swims through the stream;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਭਵ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਤਰੀਐ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: surat sabad bhav saagar tareeai naanak naam vakhaane |
lines.english: with one's consciousness focused on the Word of the Shabad, one crosses over the terrifying world-ocean. O Nanak, chant the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਮੁ ਅਗੋਚਰੁ ਦੇਖਿ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਦਾਸੋ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: agam agochar dekh dikhaae naanak taa kaa daaso |5|
lines.english: sees and inspires others to see the inaccessible, unfathomable Lord. Nanak is his slave. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਹਹਿ ਇਕਾਂਤਿ ਏਕੋ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਆਸਾ ਮਾਹਿ ਨਿਰਾਸੋ ॥
lines.translit: raheh ikaant eko man vasiaa aasaa maeh niraaso |
lines.english: One who lives alone, as a hermit, enshrining the One Lord in his mind, remaining unaffected by hope in the midst of hope,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਸੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੈ ਉਤਰੁ ਦੀਜੈ ਕਿਉ ਪਾਈਐ ਗੁਰ ਦੁਆਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: ros na keejai utar deejai kiau paaeeai gur duaaro |
lines.english: Don't be angry with us - please tell us: How can we find the Guru's Door?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਣਿ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਪੂਛਉ ਸਾਚੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: sun suaamee aradaas hamaaree poochhau saach beechaaro |
lines.english: "Listen, Lord, to our prayer. We seek your true opinion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਏ ਕਰਤਾ ਲਾਗੈ ਸਾਚਿ ਪਿਆਰੋ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: aape mel milaae karataa laagai saach piaaro |6|
lines.english: The Creator Himself unites us in Union, and inspires us to love the Truth. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਚਲਤਉ ਸਚ ਘਰਿ ਬੈਸੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਧਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man chaltau sach ghar baisai naanak naam adhaaro |
lines.english: This fickle mind sits in its true home, O Nanak, through the Support of the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਦ ਮੂਲੁ ਅਹਾਰੋ ਖਾਈਐ ਅਉਧੂ ਬੋਲੈ ਗਿਆਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: kand mool ahaaro khaaeeai aaudhoo bolai giaane |
lines.english: For food, we take fruits and roots. This is the spiritual wisdom spoken by the renunciates.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਟੀ ਬਾਟੀ ਰਹਹਿ ਨਿਰਾਲੇ ਰੂਖਿ ਬਿਰਖਿ ਉਦਿਆਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: haattee baattee raheh niraale rookh birakh udiaane |
lines.english: "Away from stores and highways, we live in the woods, among plants and trees.
